title: Mailchimp vs GetResponse (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Mailchimp and GetResponse email marketing platforms. See pricing, features, and which tool wins for your business needs.
h1: Mailchimp vs GetResponse: Complete Feature & Price Comparison
intro: Both Mailchimp and GetResponse are established email marketing platforms offering freemium models and automation capabilities. Mailchimp dominates the beginner market with its simplicity and widespread adoption, while GetResponse excels for creators and marketers who need webinars and online course hosting. Your choice depends on whether you prioritize ease-of-use or advanced marketing tools.
choose_a_if: You're a beginner looking for an intuitive, easy-to-learn email marketing platform
You want the lowest entry price and best free tier for testing
You need strong community support and extensive documentation
choose_b_if: You host webinars or create online courses as part of your business
You need advanced marketing funnels and conversion-focused tools
You're building a complete digital marketing ecosystem beyond email

faq.a: It depends on your needs. Mailchimp is better for beginners and email-focused campaigns due to its simpler interface and lower cost. GetResponse is better if you need webinars, courses, or advanced funnel-building capabilities. Both are strong platforms with different strengths.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Mailchimp or GetResponse?
faq.a: Mailchimp has a lower entry price at $13/month compared to GetResponse's $19/month for paid plans. Both offer identical free tiers with 500 contacts, making them equally accessible for testing purposes.

faq.q: Can I switch from GetResponse to Mailchimp?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms allow you to export your contact lists in standard formats. Most email marketing tools support CSV imports, making migration straightforward. However, you may lose automation workflows and custom data fields during the transfer.
